title: machine: ocipull do not error if downloaddir exists
body:
If users cancel the image download with CTRL-C for example then the blob dir will stay around. The next time we run the download we should just start the download again and not complain about the existing directory.

[NO NEW TEST NEEDED]

I had to repush for `s/NO NEW TEST/NO NEW TESTS/`
Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.

One of my oldest and most useful habits is to never, ever, ever, ever write to actual files or directories. Always write to `.tmp.$$` and use atomic rename once the tmpfile has been written with no errors. I haven't followed the entire chain here, but is it possible that we're not following that guideline somewhere?

Anyway, this looks safe enough.
/unhold

> One of my oldest and most useful habits is to never, ever, ever, ever write to actual files or directories. Always write to `.tmp.$$` and use atomic rename once the tmpfile has been written with no errors. I haven't followed the entire chain here, but is it possible that we're not following that guideline somewhere?

I can elaborate:
When pulling into an `oci:` directory, Podman is downloading each layer into a temporary file.  If the pull is being cancelled at that time, Podman will clean up the temp file.  Once a layer has been entirely downloaded, it will be written/moved to the `oci:`.  At that point, the data is complete.  However, there may be more layers coming after or metadata such as the config of the image and some further JSON data to tie things together.  When the pull is being cancelled at that time, things are not being cleaned up because the data isn't corrupted - only the image hasn't been fully pulled.  I think that is a sane behavior.
